trade.summary: Spagna and Svezia have solid bilateral trade. Spagna exports food products, vehicles, and chemicals; Svezia exports vehicles, machinery, pharmaceuticals, and telecommunications equipment. Both benefit from EU single market. IKEA, H&M, and Volvo have major Spanish operations.

diplomatic.summary: Spagna and Svezia maintain excellent diplomatic relations as EU members and now both NATO members (Svezia joined 2024). Both are democracies cooperating on Ucraina, climate, and digital governance. Different positions on some EU fiscal matters (Svezia more fiscally conservative) but broadly aligned on EU policy. Both strongly support Ucraina.

military.summary: Both NATO members since 2024. Spagna supported Svezia's NATO accession. Joint exercises within NATO frameworks. Both operate Gripen and Eurofighter aircraft respectively in NATO air policing. Spagna has NATO responsibilities in the South; Svezia in the North. Complementary roles.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. Swedish firms (IKEA, H&M, Volvo, Ericsson, Spotify) have major Spanish operations. Spanish firms (Inditex, Mango) compete in Swedish retail market. Both share EU regulatory environment though Svezia retains its own currency (krona).

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Svezia is a major renewable electricity producer (hydro, nuclear, wind). Spagna is a major renewable electricity producer (solar, wind) and LNG hub. Both cooperate on EU energy transition. Potential for indirect energy complementarity as both become renewable exporters.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific cooperation through EU Horizon programs. Spanish and Swedish universities partner extensively in life sciences, climate, physics, and environmental sciences. Erasmus exchanges are significant. Karolinska and Swedish universities collaborate with Spanish counterparts in medical research.
